messages.subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] possible error in events specification / implementation
messages.subject_clean: [R-sig-dyn-mod] possible error in events specification / implementation
messages.body_plain:
According to the documentation for using events in ode, when using the
data.frame approach (events = list(data=data.frame(...))) those events can
be specified in for different variables either by the names or positions in
the whole variable.

However, using positions does not appear to currently work.

Modifying the first example in ?events can demonstrate this:

derivs <- function(t, var, parms) {
  list(dvar = rep(0, 2))
}

yini <- c(1, 2) # change 1
times <- seq(0, 10, by = 0.1)
eventdat <- data.frame(var = c(1, 2, 2, 1), #change 2
time = c(1, 1, 5, 9),
value = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
method = c("add", "mult", "rep", "add"))

eventdat

out <- vode(func = derivs, y = yini, times = times, parms = NULL, events =
list(data = eventdat)) # now reports an error

plot(out)

Based on a cursory review of the rk.R code, it seems the check for events
assumes that y must be named (always calls the Ynames version).

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] regression plot and 10 and 90 percentiles
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

1) I think this may be the wrong mailing list for your question. Such
questions are typical for r-help.

2) The dependency of your data is nonlinear. What do you mean with
"regression line"? Do you want to get a straight line, or do you have
any nonlinear model in mind, or do you just want to get a "smooth line"?

3) What kind of "percentiles" do you mean? Is it possible, that you
really want to get confidence intervals? Or a prediction interval?

thpe


On 12/16/2014 2:12 PM, Titus Kassem wrote:
> Dear all,
>
> I have some set of data that I used to plot a simple regression
> line. However, when I try to plot two other lines which are the 10
> and 90th percentiles on the same graphic window but was unable to
> achieve due to my limited knowledge of r. Please kind help to do
> that. My data is below. I want GA to on the x-axis and EFW to be on
> the y-axis. Also attached is the txt version of the data. kindly
> help
>

messages.body_snippet:
Hi all,

I really want to get  prediction interval. I can plot the linear regression
line, however, I want two other lines, one above and one below the
regression line.
Thank you.

Titus Kassem

On We

messages.subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] R-sig-dynamic-models Digest, Vol 80, Issue 1
messages.body_plain:
On 12/17/2014 6:06 PM, Titus Kassem wrote:
> Hi all,
>
> I really want to get  prediction interval. I can plot the linear
> regression line, however, I want two other lines, one above and one
> below the regression line. Thank you.

As said, a regression "line" (i.e. a straight line) would not be valid
for your data, because the dependency is non-linear. And please consult
a statistical textbook about the difference between a confidence and a
prediction interval!

Anyway, here a standard example for the linear case, that can be found
in the R help pages, see: ?predict.lm

Thomas


## slightly modified example from predict.lm
x <- rnorm(15)
y <- x + rnorm(15)
predict(lm(y ~ x))
new <- data.frame(x = seq(-3, 3, 0.5))
predict(lm(y ~ x), new, se.fit = TRUE)
pred.w.plim <- predict(lm(y ~ x), new, interval = "prediction")
pred.w.clim <- predict(lm(y ~ x), new, interval = "confidence")
matplot(new$x, cbind(pred.w.clim, pred.w.plim[,-1]),
         lty = c(1,2,2,3,3), type = "l", ylab = "predicted y",
col=c(1,2,2,3,3))
points(y ~ x)
